item.title: From Chaos to Streamlined Publishing: My DevOps Journey with Hugo, GitHub Actions, and GitHub Pages
item.description: The rationale behind implementing CI/CD for this project Initially, it’s important to elucidate what the early stages of this project looked like, as this significantly influenced my decision to explore GitHub Actions more thoroughly and ultimately automate the deployment of this website. The development and hosting of this website, which you’re currently visiting, involve two distinct repositories. The first repository, maintained as private, is dedicated to the development and storage of my Hugo project.

item.title: High Performance Computing: Molecular Dynamics
item.description: Abstract In drug manufacturing, solvent-based methods are used for the crystallization of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). Often, the solvent interacts with the API resulting in the formation of a new solid compound, the solvate. When desolvation occurs upon heating, it might result in the formation of new solid forms with significantly different physicochemical properties. Therefore, in this work, we studied the desolvation kinetics by combining in situ powder X-ray diffraction (PXRD), all-atom molecular dynamics (MD) simulations, and macroscopic solid-state reaction kinetics modeling.

item.title: Mentoring Program
item.description: I’m very happy to be part of the mentoring program of the University of Chemistry & Technology (UCT)Prague. The program gives me the chance to help my student to find and pursue her next career entails, set long-term goals as well as to solve various study and personal challenges and obstacles. My mentee is Maria, a student at the Bioinformatics department of UCT Prague. Together we have created her mentoring path where I will be helping her with job searching, CV writing and expanding her professional network.

item.title: Name Di-confusion
item.description: This is my first post and instead of a proper introduction, I would like to clarify my name’s situation. I intend do that bacause many non-Greek friends and colleagues get confused since my name is different on official documents than how I normally write it. The situation Formal and informal I’m baptised in a Greek Orthodox Christian church (not my choice 😁) as Ιωάννης which directly translates to English as Ioannis.
